title: feat: do not subscribe BLE device online/offline state message
body:
## Title
feat: do not subscribe BLE device online/offline state message

## Description
# Why
In the issue #1251, MIoT cloud published an offline state message when the device became offline, but did not publish an online state message when the device became online. So, even xiaomi_home got properties_changed and event_occured message, the device entities was still shown unavailable.
MIoT cloud can not accurately determine the BLE device's online/offline state. Also see #1207. 

# Changed
- Do not subscribe BLE device online/offline state message. Assume that all BLE devices are online.

step_plan.summary: Modify MIoT client behavior so it does not subscribe to or act on cloud online/offline state messages for BLE devices (dids starting with 'blt.'), and avoid marking BLE devices offline on cloud disconnect, treating BLE devices as always online.

steps.operation: Skip subscribing to BLE device cloud state and avoid marking BLE devices offline on cloud disconnect
steps.reason: BLE devices (dids starting with 'blt.') receive unreliable online/offline cloud state messages (cloud may publish 'offline' without ever publishing 'online'), which causes entities to be shown unavailable even when property/event messages arrive. To prevent spurious offline state flips, do not subscribe to per-device cloud state for BLE devices when MIPS cloud connects, and do not set BLE devices' cloud 'online' to False when the MIPS cloud disconnects. This preserves existing behavior in __update_devices_from_cloud_async that forces BLE devices to be considered online.
steps.side_effects: Unit/integration tests that assert cloud state subscriptions or offline marking semantics may need updates. BLE devices will be treated as always online by this code-path, which could hide genuinely offline BLE devices if cloud state were the only source of truth; however, property/event messages will still be processed normally. No external API changes. Other non-BLE devices keep existing subscription/disconnect behavior. Downstream code that relied on cloud-state messages for BLE devices will no longer receive them (they will still work for non-BLE devices).
